name: Security Evidence Evaluation Rubric
instructions: Evaluate the security analysis for evidence quality, severity calibration, and actionable accuracy.

metrics.evidence_grounding: Rate 1-5. Score 5 if every finding links to a specific file path, class name, and evidence from the topography. Score 1 if descriptions are generic or reference files not in the map.
metrics.hallucination_count: Count how many non-existent code files, components, or variables were invented by the model.
metrics.severity_calibration: Rate 1-5. Score 5 if every HIGH/CRITICAL finding is fully justified by visible evidence and would not be a false positive upon code inspection. Score 3 if some findings are over-severed (e.g., marking methods as HIGH when they could be stubs). Score 1 if multiple findings are clearly over-stated or misclassified given the available parser data.
metrics.actionable_finding_rate: Rate 1-5. Score 5 if 80%+ of findings would realistically warrant developer attention. Score 3 if half are false positives or informational noise. Score 1 if nearly all findings are invalid due to parser gaps (stubs, custom auth, missing attributes).
